     The Numbers Business Report

End of Watch (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $41,003,371Details
International Box Office $16,583,235
Worldwide Box Office $57,586,606
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $16,820,608
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $9,438,719
Total $26,259,327
Further financial details...

Synopsis

End Of Watch follows two young Los Angeles police officers Taylor and Zavala as they patrol the mean streets of south central Los Angeles. Giving the story a gripping, first-person immediacy, the action unfolds through footage from the handheld HD cameras of the police officers, gang members, surveillance cameras, and citizens caught in the line of fire to create a riveting portrait of the city's most dangerous corners, the cops who risk their lives there every day, and the price they and their families are forced to pay.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$13,152,683 (32.1% of total gross)
Legs:3.12 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:71.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$15,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,730 opening theaters/2,780 max. theaters, 4.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $61,600,650

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 21st, 2012 (Wide) by Open Road
International Releases: September 20th, 2012 (Wide) (Ukraine)
September 20th, 2012 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
September 21st, 2012 (Wide) (Turkey)
September 27th, 2012 (Wide) (Thailand)
September 28th, 2012 (Wide) (Taiwan)
Video Release: January 22nd, 2013 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong violence, some disturbing images, pervasive language including sexual references, and some drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2234, 8/1/2012)
Running Time: 109 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Epilogue, Filmed By a Character, Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Beat Cops, Buddy Cop, Gangs, Narcotics, Surprise Twist
Social setting: Hood Film, Human-Trafficking, Organized Crime
Subgenre: Crime Thriller
Target audience: Hispanic
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Open Road Films, Exclusive Media Group, EFF-Hedge Fund Films, Le Grisbi, Crave Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, Spanish

Synopsis

End Of Watch follows two young Los Angeles police officers Taylor and Zavala as they patrol the mean streets of south central Los Angeles. Giving the story a gripping, first-person immediacy, the action unfolds through footage from the handheld HD cameras of the police officers, gang members, surveillance cameras, and citizens caught in the line of fire to create a riveting portrait of the city's most dangerous corners, the cops who risk their lives there every day, and the price they and their families are forced to pay.
